_readme: HUMAN-ONLY. This is the only file in the harness that can GRANT completion,
and no agent may write it - forbid-gate-tamper blocks agent Edit/Write and
any shell command that would modify it. Thomas ticks cells here in his own
editor after looking at the contact sheet.
Why a human owns this axis: 'is this surface good?' is a taste judgement, and
the measured evidence says a vision model cannot make it. UI-Lens (CVPR 2026)
puts MLLM F1 at 20.4% on text overflow and 31.2% on collision; arXiv 2510.08783
puts pairwise UI-quality accuracy near chance when two screens are close in
quality; arXiv 2606.13685 needs >=11 trials for 95% reliability where this
harness used 2. The judge stays, but as a defect detector that can only veto.
To tick a cell, add an entry keyed exactly as the oracle prints it:
The signature pin means a tick expires the moment the surface changes again:
sign-off applies to what you actually looked at, not to the surface forever.
